title: Numerical stability and error amplification
description: A scheme is stable when errors stay bounded over time rather than growing, which depends on step size and the method.
body: Use when reasoning about why a simulation blows up. Do not confuse stability with accuracy, since a stable scheme can still be inaccurate and an accurate-looking step can be unstable. Actions: relate stability to whether the per-step error amplification factor stays bounded, check the step against the stability region, and watch for unbounded growth. Validation signals include a bounded solution over long times and error that does not amplify each step. Risk boundary: an unstable scheme produces meaningless results that may look plausible early, so flag any unbounded growth as a stability failure, not a tuning issue.
